note: drafted in the build session from the live packet — awaiting review
text: If today's north wind felt like a gift, that was the cold front clearing out the week's soup, and the weekend behind it is the good stuff. This afternoon is the only wrinkle: the official forecast has a few isolated showers after 4, and the European model is running much wetter than the American one, so keep half an eye on the sky if you're on the water late. Otherwise it's low 80s, dry, and the lake is sitting at 70 with waves a foot or less into Sunday, about as friendly as the broad lake gets. One caveat: Canadian wildfire smoke has air quality in the moderate range, so if you're sensitive, take the easy version of tonight's run. Sunset's 8:38 and worth a walk. Saturday and Sunday look like the two best lake days in a while; I'd get the swim in this weekend, because Tuesday turns hot with real storm potential behind it.

WCAX METEOROLOGIST: BURLINGTON, Vt. (WCAX) - A cold front is finishing its push through the region today, ending our recent shower activity and bringing in a much more comfortable air mass for the weekend. Aside from a stray shower today, mainly over the Adirondacks or Green Mountains, most of the region trends dry with clearing skies and lower humidity. The weekend looks fantastic — sunny, warm, and comfortable. Heat builds again early next week, peaking Tuesday with highs in the mid-90s and a First Alert Weather Day in effect for possible strong storms as a powerful cold front approaches. Behind that front, more seasonable and comfortable summer weather returns by the middle and end of next week.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-09T22:03:00Z): Beautiful weekend, less humid — Turning less humid Friday, beautiful weekend ahead Beautiful weekend, less humid Turning less humid Friday, beautiful weekend ahead
